pairs.question: When was Grant Mulvey born?
pairs.answer: Grant 'Granny' Mulvey was born on September 17, 1956.

pairs.question: What NHL team did Grant Mulvey play for during most of his career?
pairs.answer: Grant Mulvey played for the Chicago Black Hawks (now known as the Chicago Blackhawks) for all but 12 games of his 586-game NHL career.

pairs.question: How old was Grant Mulvey when he scored his first NHL goal, and who broke his record?
pairs.answer: Grant Mulvey scored his first NHL goal at the age of 18 years and 32 days. Aleksander Barkov surpassed this record in 2013 by scoring a goal at 18 years and 31 days.

pairs.question: What franchise record did Grant Mulvey set on February 3, 1982, in a game against the St. Louis Blues?
pairs.answer: On February 3, 1982, Grant Mulvey scored five goals and added two assists for seven points in a single game, setting a franchise record for most goals in a game. He also tied the record for most goals in a single period with four goals.

pairs.question: What role does Grant Mulvey currently hold with the Chicago Blackhawks Alumni Association?
pairs.answer: Grant Mulvey is an active board member and the Treasurer for the Chicago Blackhawks Alumni Association.

pairs.question: What honor did Grant Mulvey receive in 2020?
pairs.answer: Grant Mulvey was inducted into the Illinois Hockey Hall of Fame in 2020.

pairs.question: How does Grant Mulvey contribute to the community outside of his hockey career?
pairs.answer: Grant Mulvey supports many charities in Chicago, giving back to the community as many former athletes do in the city.
